steps.title: Identify the opening signal
steps.content: The text begins with “I HEAR it is charged against me that I seek to destroy institutions” — this signals that the speaker is directly addressing a criticism that has been leveled against him.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The speaker first refutes this charge with “really I am neither for nor against institutions,” then shifts with “Only I will establish...” to announce his grand ambition: creating “The institution of the dear love of comrades.” This structure—addressing a criticism directed at him, then announcing an ambitious goal—matches choice D exactly.

The other three either misidentify the opening as questioning attitudes rather than responding to a charge against the speaker, or introduce emotions and claims not present in the text.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
